From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Rugby union competition in England

This article is about the rugby union competition. For the rugby league competition formerly known as National League One, see [Championship (rugby league)](/wiki/Championship_(rugby_league) "Championship (rugby league)").

**National One**, up until 2023 known as **National League 1** and previously known before September 2009 as **National Division Two**), is the third of three national leagues in the domestic [rugby union](/wiki/Rugby_union "Rugby union") competition of England. It was known as **Courage League National Division Three** when it was founded in 1987.[[1]](#cite_note-Rothmans-1) [Richmond](/wiki/Richmond_R.F.C. "Richmond R.F.C.") are the current champions.

The [Rugby Football Union](/wiki/Rugby_Football_Union "Rugby Football Union") (RFU) approved a new structure for the National Leagues from the 2022–23 season. This division was reduced to fourteen teams, playing each other on a home and away basis to make a total of 26 matches each.

## Structure

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_League_1&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Structure")]

The league consists of fourteen teams, with all the teams playing each other on a home and away basis, to make a total of twenty-six matches each. For the 2025–26 season there is one automatic promotion place, with the champions going up to [Champ Rugby](/wiki/Champ_Rugby "Champ Rugby"), while the runners up will play the 13th placed Champ Rugby side in a playoff for a possible second promotion place.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Clubs finishing 12th, 13th and 14th will automatically be relegated to either, [National League 2 East](/wiki/National_League_2_East "National League 2 East"), [National League 2 North](/wiki/National_League_2_North "National League 2 North") or [National League 2 West](/wiki/National_League_2_West "National League 2 West"), depending on the geographical location of the team. The 11th placed side will enter a four team playoff with the runners up of the three tier 4 leagues, with one side going up/remaining in National League 1.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The results of the matches contribute points to the league table as follows:

* 4 points are awarded for a win
* 2 points are awarded for a draw
* 0 points are awarded for a loss, however
* 1 losing (bonus) point is awarded to a team that loses a match by 7 points or fewer
* 1 additional (bonus) point is awarded to a team scoring 4 tries or more in a match.

Rules for classification: If teams are level at any stage, tiebreakers are applied in the following order:

1. Number of matches won
2. Number of matches drawn
3. Difference between points for and against
4. Total number of points for
5. Aggregate number of points scored in matches between tied teams
6. Number of matches won excluding the first match, then the second and so on until the tie is settled

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_League_1&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: History")]

When the rugby union leagues were introduced in 1987 the division was known as Courage League National Division Three. Ten years later, in 1997, the league was restructured and the Premiership was introduced, which consisted of the top two divisions. Therefore, National 3 became the top league outside of the Premiership structure, and was renamed as National 1. In 2000–01, the Premiership was reduced to a single division and National 1 was renamed National 2. Following the formation of the professional [RFU Championship](/wiki/RFU_Championship "RFU Championship") in 2009 the league, once again, became known as National League 1, and is currently the lowest tier that is nationwide. The league has previously consisted of fourteen clubs, but from 2009 to 2010 increased to sixteen before reducing to fourteen again ahead of the 2022–23 season. Only one team was promoted to the RFU Championship and between 2009–10 and 2019–20 three teams were relegated to either [National League 2 North](/wiki/National_League_2_North "National League 2 North") or [National League 2 South](/wiki/National_League_2_South "National League 2 South") depending on geographical location. Following reorganisation in 2022, three teams are now relegated to either [National League 2 East](/wiki/National_League_2_East "National League 2 East"), [National League 2 North](/wiki/National_League_2_North "National League 2 North") or [National League 2 West](/wiki/National_League_2_West "National League 2 West") depending on geographical location.

## Records

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_League_1&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Records")]

*Note that all records are from 1996–97 season onwards as this is widely held as the dawn of professionalism across the English club game. It also offers a better comparison between seasons as the division team numbers are roughly equal (for example when league rugby union first started in 1987–88 the Courage League National Division Three had only 12 teams playing 11 games each, compared to 16 teams in 1996–97 playing 30 games (home & away)). Attendance records are from 2000 onwards unless otherwise specified. All records are up to date up till the end of the 2019–20 season.*

## Notes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_League_1&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Notes")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-12)** Typically the 12th placed team would be relegated but [Leicester Lions](/wiki/Leicester_Lions_RFC "Leicester Lions RFC") avoided relegation from the [2024–25 National League 1](/wiki/2024%E2%80%9325_National_League_1 "2024–25 National League 1") due to the [RFU Championship](/wiki/RFU_Championship "RFU Championship") expanding from 12 to 14 teams ahead of the 2025–26 season.[[11]](#cite_note-11)
2. **[^](#cite_ref-25)** [Sheffield](/wiki/Sheffield_RUFC "Sheffield RUFC"), [Leeds](/wiki/Leeds_Tykes "Leeds Tykes"), [Liverpool St Helens](/wiki/Liverpool_St_Helens_F.C. "Liverpool St Helens F.C."), [Clifton](/wiki/Clifton_Rugby_Football_Club "Clifton Rugby Football Club"), [Aspatria](/wiki/Aspatria_RUFC "Aspatria RUFC"), [Askeans](/w/index.php?title=Askeans&action=edit&redlink=1 "Askeans (page does not exist)"), [Broughton Park](/wiki/Broughton_Park_RUFC "Broughton Park RUFC") and [Plymouth Albion](/wiki/Plymouth_Albion "Plymouth Albion") were the relegated teams. The large number of relegations was due to the restructuring of the league system for the 1993–94 season.
3. **[^](#cite_ref-29)** [Rugby](/wiki/Rugby_Lions "Rugby Lions") and [Rotherham](/wiki/Rotherham_R.U.F.C. "Rotherham R.U.F.C.") were also promoted.
4. **[^](#cite_ref-32)** [London Welsh](/wiki/London_Welsh_RFC "London Welsh RFC") and [Rugby](/wiki/Rugby_Lions "Rugby Lions") were also promoted.
5. **[^](#cite_ref-49)** Only 2 teams relegated at the end of the 2016–17 season instead of 3 due to [London Welsh](/wiki/London_Welsh_RFC "London Welsh RFC") being expelled from the [RFU Championship](/wiki/RFU_Championship "RFU Championship") in January 2017.[[44]](#cite_note-londonwelshliquidation-48)
6. **[^](#cite_ref-52)** Due to the [COVID-19 pandemic in the United Kingdom](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic_in_the_United_Kingdom "COVID-19 pandemic in the United Kingdom") the [RFU](/wiki/Rugby_Football_Union "Rugby Football Union") cancelled all rugby below the [Premiership](/wiki/Premiership_Rugby "Premiership Rugby") with most clubs in National League 1 having played 25 games, and a best playing record formula was used to decide the final table.[[45]](#cite_note-50)[[46]](#cite_note-51)
7. **[^](#cite_ref-56)** The expansion of the [2025–26 RFU Championship](/wiki/2025%E2%80%9326_RFU_Championship "2025–26 RFU Championship") from 12 to 14 teams meant only 2 teams were relegated instead of 3 meaning that 12th placed [Leicester Lions](/wiki/Leicester_Lions_RFC "Leicester Lions RFC") stayed in National League 1.
8. **[^](#cite_ref-58)** Penzance & Newlyn are now known as the Cornish Pirates.
9. **[^](#cite_ref-60)** Note that due to poor attendance keeping by press and online sources means that the 2000–01 and 2003–04 seasons are excluded from these statistics due to lack of information expect in the case of lowest recorded league game attendance.
